title: Apple Cider Blondies
ingredients_html:
Blondies

  • 2 cups apple cider
  • 1/2 cup melted butter (113g)
  • 1 cup brown sugar (200g)
  • 1 egg + 1 egg yolk
  • 1 tsp vanilla extract
  • 1 cup all-purpose flour (126g)
  • 1/4 tsp baking powder
  • 1/4 tsp kosher salt
  • 1/2 tsp cinnamon

Topping

  • 1/3 cup white granulated sugar (66g)
  • 1 tsp cinnamon
steps_html:
1. Place a 2 quart saucepan over medium-high heat and add in apple cider. Bring to a simmer, stirring occasionally. After about 30 minutes, the apple cider should be reduced to about half. Increase heat to come to a rolling boil and continue to stir, about 5 minutes. Turn heat down to low and check on reduction, there should only be about a 1/4 cup of syrup. Set aside.
  2. Preheat oven to 350°F. Line an 8 x 8 baking pan with parchment paper.
  3. In a large bowl, combine melted butter and brown sugar.
  4. Mix in eggs and vanilla extract.
  5. Mix in apple cider reduction until smooth.
  6. Add in flour, salt, baking powder, and cinnamon. Mix until combined and pour mixture into prepared pan.
  7. In a small bowl, combine sugar and cinnamon and mix thoroughly.
  8. Sprinkle cinnamon sugar mixture over the batter in the pan.
  9. Bake for 28 to 30 minutes, or until the edges are fully set. Use a toothpick to check if done.
  10. Remove pan from oven and allow to cool for 20 minutes.
notes_html: • For a more fudgy blondie instead of cakey, bake for less time.
